name: Herb Ellis
abstract: Mitchell Herbert Ellis (August 4, 1921 – March 28, 2010) was an American jazz guitarist. During the 1950s, he was in a trio with pianist Oscar Peterson.

interviews.title: Herb Ellis interviewed by Michael Woods, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]
Herb Ellis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: Oscar asked me would I join the trio... he told me that Herb Ellis had taken sick and he needed a replacement, and could I join him right away... Herb Ellis got himself together and came back.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: So I hired the best rhythm section I could find: Colin Bailey was a monster drummer, Monty Budwig who was a monster bass player, Mike Melvoin a monster piano player, and Herb Ellis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 5, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Brown
about_quotes.evidence: Herb Ellis and myself and Buddy Rich or Louis Bellson... Herb Ellis on guitar; Oscar Peterson on piano; Ray Brown on bass
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Brown interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, AZ, April 16, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Smulyan
about_quotes.evidence: And there was a band called the Philip Morris Superband... And it was Ray Brown and Jeff Hamilton played the first year on drums, and James Moody and Herb Ellis and Urbie Green... I mean it was an amazing band. And we went on the road for three months.

about_quotes.speaker: Oscar Peterson
about_quotes.evidence: Many of his recording feature the legendary trio with Ray Brown and Barney Kessel (later Herb Ellis and Ed Thigpen).
about_quotes.doc_title: Oscar Peterson interviewed by Joe Williams, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: they had what became later the, Soft Winds, Herb Ellis was playing guitar, that’s where I ran into Herbie.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
